output.value:
### Adoption > Incremental Adoption

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

Each component in Radix UI is its own independently versioned package, allowing for incremental adoption into your project. This approach means you don't need to perform a massive rewrite of your codebase to start using Radix components. Instead, you can begin with a single component and gradually add more components alongside your existing code as your project evolves.

--------------------------------

### Developer Resources > Documentation and Support

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

Radix documentation provides comprehensive support for developers including real-world examples, extensive API references, and detailed accessibility information. Full TypeScript support is included across all components, ensuring type safety and better developer tooling. The consistent API design across components creates a unified developer experience that makes working with Radix Primitives intuitive and enjoyable.

--------------------------------

### Design Philosophy > Consistent API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

Components with similar functionality share similar API patterns in Radix UI. This consistency means that once you learn how to use one component, you can apply that knowledge to other components with similar purposes. The shared API design creates a predictable and intuitive developer experience across the entire component library.

--------------------------------

### Develop with an open, thought‑out API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

One of our main goals is to provide the best possible developer experience. Radix Primitives provides a fully-typed API. All components share a similar API, creating a consistent and predictable experience.

--------------------------------

### Why Radix Primitives > Spend less time on undifferentiated work

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

It takes _a lot_ of time to develop and maintain a robust set of UI components, and it's mostly undifferentiated work. Building on top of Radix components will save you time and money, so you can ship a better product faster. It’s no secret that robust UI components are tricky to build. Nailing accessibility details and complex logic sucks time away from product feature development. With Radix, you can focus on your unique engineering challenges instead.

Prompt instructions file:
## Plan: React Todo App with Radix UI

Create a modern, accessible Todo application using React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and Radix UI primitives. The app will feature task management, filtering, and a dark/light theme toggle, following the provided UI design.

### Steps
1. Initialize a React TypeScript project using Vite and install Radix UI primitives (`checkbox`, `dialog`, `select`, `label`) and `lucide-react` for icons.
2. Set up Tailwind CSS for styling, including custom configurations for the specific purple/blue accents shown in the [design](https://placeholder-link-to-image).
3. Create reusable UI components in `src/components/ui/` by wrapping Radix primitives like `Checkbox` and `Dialog`.
4. Implement the main [App.tsx](src/App.tsx) layout containing the search bar, filter `Select` component, and theme toggle.
5. Build the `TodoItem` component using Radix `Checkbox` and `Label`, with strikethrough styling for completed tasks.
6. Develop the task creation flow using a Radix `Dialog` triggered by the floating action button.

### Further Considerations
1. State management: Should we use simple `useState` / `useReducer` or a library like Zustand? Recommended: Zustand for cleaner list operations.
2. Persistence: Should tasks persist after refresh? Recommendation: Use `localStorage` to save the todo list state.
3. Animation: Should we add subtle transitions for adding/removing tasks? Recommendation: Use Framer Motion for the list enter/exit animations.

</attachment>
Follow instructions in [plan-reactTodoAppRadixUI.prompt.md](untitled:plan-reactTodoAppRadixUI.prompt.md).

{"content":"# React Todo App with Radix UI

A modern, accessible Todo application built with React, Vite, Tailwind CSS (v4), and Radix UI primitives.

## Features

- **Task Management**: Create, toggle, and delete tasks.
- **Filtering**: Filter tasks by "All", "Active", or "Completed".
- **Search**: Real-time search by task title.
- **Theme Support**: Dark and light mode toggle.
- **Persistence**: Tasks are saved to `localStorage`.
- **Animations**: Smooth transitions using Framer Motion.
- **Accessibility**: Built with Radix UI primitives for high accessibility.

## Tech Stack

- **Framework**: [React](https://reactjs.org/)
- **Build Tool**: [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/)
- **Styling**: [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/)
- **State Management**: [Zustand](https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand)
- **UI Components**: [Radix UI](https://www.radix-ui.com/)
- **Icons**: [Lucide React](https://lucide.dev/)
- **Animations**: [Framer Motion](https://www.framer.com/motion/)

## Getting Started

1. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```
2. Start the development server:
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```
3. Build for production:
   ```bash
   npm run build
   ```

The React Todo application has been successfully created with the following features and technologies:

### 🚀 Tech Stack
- **React 19** with **TypeScript** & **Vite**
- **Tailwind CSS v4** for modern, high-performance styling
- **Radix UI Primitives** for accessible UI components (`Checkbox`, `Dialog`, `Select`, `Label`)
- **Zustand** for clean and persistent state management
- **Framer Motion** for smooth task list animations
- **Lucide React** for consistent iconography

### ✨ Implemented Features
- **Task Management**: Seamlessly add, toggle, and delete tasks.
- **Persistence**: Your tasks are automatically saved to `localStorage`.
- **Filtering & Search**: Quickly find tasks using the search bar or filter by completion status.
- **Theme Support**: Integrated dark and light mode toggle that adapts to your preference.
- **Accessibility**: ARIA-compliant components built on Radix UI.

### 📁 Project Structure
- [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx): Main layout and application logic.
- [src/store/useStore.ts](src/store/useStore.ts): Zustand store for tasks and theme management.
- [src/components/TodoItem.tsx](src/components/TodoItem.tsx): Individual task component with animations.
- [src/components/ui/](src/components/ui/): Reusable Radix UI wrapper components.

### 🛠️ Getting Started
To run the application locally, use the following commands in your terminal:

1. **Install dependencies**:
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```
2. **Start the development server**:
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```

You can now start managing your tasks in a modern and accessible environment!

### Adoption > Incremental Adoption

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

Each component in Radix UI is its own independently versioned package, allowing for incremental adoption into your project. This approach means you don't need to perform a massive rewrite of your codebase to start using Radix components. Instead, you can begin with a single component and gradually add more components alongside your existing code as your project evolves.

--------------------------------

### Developer Resources > Documentation and Support

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

Radix documentation provides comprehensive support for developers including real-world examples, extensive API references, and detailed accessibility information. Full TypeScript support is included across all components, ensuring type safety and better developer tooling. The consistent API design across components creates a unified developer experience that makes working with Radix Primitives intuitive and enjoyable.

--------------------------------

### Design Philosophy > Consistent API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

Components with similar functionality share similar API patterns in Radix UI. This consistency means that once you learn how to use one component, you can apply that knowledge to other components with similar purposes. The shared API design creates a predictable and intuitive developer experience across the entire component library.

--------------------------------

### Develop with an open, thought‑out API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

One of our main goals is to provide the best possible developer experience. Radix Primitives provides a fully-typed API. All components share a similar API, creating a consistent and predictable experience.

--------------------------------

### Why Radix Primitives > Spend less time on undifferentiated work

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/docs/primitives

It takes _a lot_ of time to develop and maintain a robust set of UI components, and it's mostly undifferentiated work. Building on top of Radix components will save you time and money, so you can ship a better product faster. It’s no secret that robust UI components are tricky to build. Nailing accessibility details and complex logic sucks time away from product feature development. With Radix, you can focus on your unique engineering challenges instead.

The React Todo application has been successfully created with the following features and technologies:

### 🚀 Tech Stack
- **React 19** with **TypeScript** & **Vite**
- **Tailwind CSS v4** for modern, high-performance styling
- **Radix UI Primitives** for accessible UI components (`Checkbox`,

, `Label`)
- **Zustand** for clean and persistent state management
- **Framer Motion** for smooth task list animations
- **Lucide React** for consistent iconography

### ✨ Implemented Features
- **Task Management**: Seamlessly add, toggle, and delete tasks.
- **Persistence**: Your tasks are automatically saved to `localStorage`.
- **Filtering & Search**: Quickly find tasks using the search bar or filter by completion status.
- **Theme Support**: Integrated dark and light mode toggle that adapts to your preference.
- **Accessibility**: ARIA-compliant components built on Radix UI.

### 📁 Project Structure
-

: Zustand store for tasks and theme management.
-

: Reusable Radix UI wrapper components.

### 🛠️ Getting Started
To run the application locally, use the following commands in your terminal:

1. **Install dependencies**:
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```
2. **Start the development server**:
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```

You can now start managing your tasks in a modern and accessible environment!
